title: Marketplace Briefing: AI traffic is poised to reshape the Amazon funnel
gist: Amazon agency Envision Horizons launched a beta AI visibility tracker to measure how referral traffic from ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini influences purchases on Amazon, reflecting growing agency interest in understanding generative AI's role in the shopping funnel.

title: Meta introduces AI-powered room visualization feature
gist: Meta AI now lets shoppers upload photos of their rooms and visualize catalog products placed inside them, pulling from existing ad catalog data, with the ability to compare options and purchase directly through the brand's website.

title: Back-to-school shoppers head to mass merchants, department stores
gist: Deloitte's 2026 back-to-school survey projects flat spending at $557 per child (down 6% inflation-adjusted), with mass merchants dominating and 57% of parents expecting the economy to worsen — but parents using generative AI tools plan to spend the most, averaging $737 per child.

title: To manage 300,000 creators, Unilever automates everything but the relationship
gist: Unilever scaled its creator program from 10,000 to 300,000 influencers and is now automating discovery, vetting, and content approvals while keeping human judgment at the center of actual relationship management.

title: Phia accused of ‘cookie stuffing,’ taking affiliate credit on purchases it didn’t earn
gist: Phia, a shopping browser extension that finds lowest prices and discount codes, has been accused of 'cookie stuffing' — secretly opening background tabs to override affiliate referral codes from other sources and claim commissions on purchases it didn't drive. The company was suspended from affiliate platform Impact.com; it says the issue has been fixed.

title: Brands won this season of ‘Love Island USA’
gist: Love Island USA's season 8 drew 1.31 billion viewing minutes in its first week (up 69% YoY), and ad sales across the show and its spinoff rose 73% versus last season, with over 1,000 media partners and seven integrated brand sponsors. Brands are increasingly integrating into the show's content rather than running traditional ads.

title: New York City to ban deceptive subscription practices
gist: New York City is banning deceptive subscription practices starting October 1, requiring easy cancellation and imposing fines of $525 per affected subscription; it is also proposing rules that mandate total upfront price disclosure, including all mandatory fees. The subscription rule could save NYC residents an estimated $162.5 million per year.

title: How Aéropostale entertains Gen Alpha with a creator-led mini-series
gist: Aéropostale launched a creator-led serialized mini-series ('Intern Diaries') starring TikTok creator Déjà Clark, pulling 3.5 million views on its first two episodes; a tied exclusive app-first collection drop strategy drove 30% YoY customer growth and previously catapulted the brand's app into the App Store top 5. The brand is also linking loyalty programs with J.C. Penney to drive cross-shopping.

title: Disable autoplay and infinite scroll or risk massive fines, EU tells Meta
gist: The European Commission has preliminarily found that Meta's features — autoplay, infinite scroll, and highly personalized recommendations — violate the Digital Services Act by creating addictive design without adequate risk assessment, and is ordering changes or fines up to 6% of global turnover.

title: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive features on Facebook and Instagram
gist: The EU found Meta in breach of the Digital Services Act over addictive design features including infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications, and personalized recommendation algorithms, and is demanding Meta disable these features by default and reduce engagement-maximizing algorithmic behavior or face fines of up to 6% of global turnover.

title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
gist: Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI alleging that former Apple employees, including OpenAI's Chief Hardware Officer Tang Tan, systematically stole trade secrets — including hardware specs, prototypes, and proprietary manufacturing techniques — to benefit OpenAI's nascent hardware business.

title: Apple sues OpenAI, accuses ex-employees of stealing trade secrets
gist: Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI details allegations that former Apple executives and engineers at OpenAI used job interviews to extract confidential hardware information, coached departing employees to evade Apple's security protocols, and used Apple's proprietary manufacturing techniques without authorization — with over 400 former Apple employees now at OpenAI.

title: Nvidia, CoreWeave, and Nebius: Inside the Circular Financing of the GPU Boom
gist: Analysis of CoreWeave and Nebius reveals a circular financing structure where Nvidia invests in both neoclouds, which then buy billions in Nvidia GPUs, while hyperscalers like Microsoft and Meta commit over $120B to these companies partly to shift capex off their balance sheets. Both neoclouds face large funding gaps as capex runs 2:1 ahead of operating cash flow, driving rapidly rising debt loads.

title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
gist: OpenAI is hiring a product manager dedicated to families, caregivers, and older adults, as data shows ChatGPT's 35+ user share rose from 26% to 31% in a year and nearly one in four U.S. parent smartphone users now use the product. The move signals a shift from individual productivity tool toward household-level AI, bringing new trust and safety requirements.

title: Private label: Not just a price driven choice anymore
gist: Private labels have evolved from low-cost generics to quality-competitive products spanning premium, health, and niche categories, with retailers using supply chain control and dynamic pricing to outmargin branded competitors. The article projects further evolution through AI-driven personalization and sustainable practices.

title: After the click: Turning AI-referred shoppers into loyal customers
gist: AI-referred shoppers convert 42% better and spend more per visit than other traffic channels, but they arrive with high expectations set by conversational AI — slow sites, poor on-site search, and generic experiences quickly erase that advantage. Retailers are being pushed to match the intent-aware, conversational quality of the AI assistants that sent shoppers their way.

title: DTC brands are finding growth with older customers
gist: Several DTC brands, including baby food startup Little Spoon and charcuterie delivery service Boarderie, are discovering that older demographics — grandparents and Baby Boomers — are driving meaningful growth, prompting shifts in marketing, messaging, and product development.

title: Intent Clusters Guide AI Product Discovery
gist: As AI assistants handle longer, more context-rich shopping queries than traditional search, e-commerce marketers can build 'product intent clusters' — networks of scenario-specific content pages linked to a central product detail page — to influence AI-generated recommendations. Generative AI makes it economically viable to produce and maintain the large volume of narrow-use-case pages this strategy requires.

title: Retailers stock up ahead of expected tariff changes
gist: U.S. port import volumes are forecast to hit an all-time record in July as retailers frontload inventory before expected new tariffs in August, following the expiration of the 10% Section 122 global tariff on July 24. August volumes are then projected to drop 4.5% year-over-year as the pull-forward effect fades.

title: Ports brace for record-high imports in July
gist: U.S. ports are projected to handle an all-time high of 2.47 million TEUs in July, driven by retailers rushing to import goods ahead of anticipated tariff increases, per NRF and Hackett Associates data.

title: Amazon Shipping looks to snag FedEx, UPS customers with low pricing
gist: Amazon Shipping is aggressively undercutting FedEx and UPS rates — offering savings of up to 33% for some large retail clients — as it expands its parcel delivery service beyond Amazon sellers, with no residential surcharges or weekend delivery fees. The service currently covers ground shipping only and is best suited for high-volume retailers with lightweight packages.

title: Retailers did well in Q1. Was it just a mirage?
gist: Retail analysts warn that strong Q1 results at many retailers were inflated by soft prior-year comps, early Easter timing, and tariff-driven price increases that hadn't yet hit consumer resistance — with Q3 2025 expected to reveal which retailers have genuine momentum versus those that simply rode favorable circumstances.

title: MediaMarktSaturn wants to double marketplace GMV by 2029
gist: Ceconomy has announced a strategy for MediaMarktSaturn to more than double its marketplace GMV from €800 million to over €1.6 billion by the 2028–2029 financial year.

title: SharkNinja’s new growth strategy runs through comedy creators
gist: SharkNinja CMO Kaitlyn Hebert is shifting the brand's creator strategy toward comedy-focused content to reach audiences beyond the gym-goers and parents who already drove viral success for products like the Ninja Creami.

title: How a NYC chef turned a takeout window into a national subscription brand with 10,000 orders a month
gist: Bone broth brand Brodo closed four of five Manhattan locations and rebuilt around a DTC subscription model on WooCommerce, now processing 10,000 orders per month nationally with self-service subscription management (pause, skip, swap) credited as the key retention mechanism.

title: New GA4 Channel Tracks AI Traffic
gist: Google Analytics 4 added an 'AI Assistant' channel in May that tracks traffic from generative AI platforms including ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude, allowing site owners to measure engagement and identify which pages are being cited in AI-generated answers.

title: In customer service, third-party generative AI tools are beating brand chatbots
gist: A Gartner survey of 3,500+ customers found that consumers are three times more likely to use third-party AI tools like ChatGPT than a brand's own chatbot for customer service, with third-party AI usage doubling in the past year while brand chatbot adoption has not grown since 2022.

title: Why retailers are losing customers after they’ve already won them
gist: Retailers invest heavily in pre-purchase experience but cede control of the post-purchase journey to third-party carriers, resulting in inconsistent, unbranded experiences that erode loyalty and waste acquisition spend. The article argues that owning post-purchase communications and carrier orchestration is now a key retention lever.

title: Publisher ad supply fell by up to 40% in Q2 as AI search choked the open web
gist: Publisher ad supply dropped 32–41% year-over-year in Q2 2026 in the US and UK, driven by AI-powered zero-click search reducing traffic to open-web sites, though rising eCPMs have partially offset the revenue impact.

title: Ingredient-led brands are dominating AI beauty citations
gist: Ingredient-focused skin-care brands like The Ordinary, CeraVe, and La Roche-Posay are earning the most AI citations in beauty, with The Ordinary appearing in 7% of beauty-related AI responses across ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews.

title: European retail media increased 16.7% in 2025
gist: European digital ad spend grew 10.5% in 2025 to €131.1 billion, with retail media outpacing the broader market at 16.7% growth and reaching €13.3 billion.

title: Caraway debuts cookware in over 500 Walmart stores
gist: DTC cookware brand Caraway is expanding into over 500 Walmart stores nationwide, its largest physical retail push to date, following earlier partnerships with Target and The Container Store.

title: Amazon announces 2026 holiday fulfillment fees, advises early shipping
gist: Amazon will apply peak fulfillment fee surcharges averaging $0.32 per unit from October 15, 2026 to January 14, 2027, on top of a 3.5% fuel and logistics surcharge that has no expiration date, and is advising sellers to stock inventory by October for holiday readiness.

title: Whatnot acquires Shaped to power real-time live shopping recommendations
gist: Whatnot has acquired Shaped, an ML company specializing in real-time recommendation and search, to bring recommendations for live auctions closer to real-time as inventory and buyer intent shift by the second.

title: The state of agentic storefronts: How AI agents guide shoppers on frictionless, full-funnel journeys
gist: A Glossy/Swap survey of 80 brands, retailers, and agencies found 90% already use AI to improve shopping experiences, with Salesforce data crediting AI agents with driving 20% of retail sales and $262B in holiday spend during the 2025 season.

title: Salomon continues its US growth streak by launching at Foot Locker
gist: Salomon is launching at Foot Locker on July 19 and will co-host a series of farmers'-market-inspired pop-ups in New York, Los Angeles, and Miami to support the partnership.

title: Walmart bets on AI and digital twins to shape its supply chain strategy
gist: Walmart's supply chain technology team uses AI agents, digital twin simulations, and predictive models to optimize inventory routing, balance cost and speed, and respond rapidly to disruptions like facility closures or sudden demand shifts.

title: Inflation slows to 3.5% as Warsh pledges to achieve Fed’s 2% target
gist: US consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, down from 4.2% in May, driven largely by a 5.7% drop in energy prices, though the Fed's new chair signaled rate hikes remain on the table if inflation doesn't continue to cool.

title: Stripe and Advent reportedly offered to buy PayPal for around $53.4B
gist: Stripe and Advent International have jointly bid approximately $53.4B to acquire PayPal, backed by ~$50B in bank financing, which would combine two platforms processing a combined $3.7 trillion in annual payment volume.

title: WTF is LLM honeypotting?
gist: Publishers and e-commerce brands are deploying 'LLM honeypots' — fake content mazes designed to waste AI crawler compute and pollute training data — as a countermeasure against unauthorized scraping. The tactic is borrowed from cybersecurity but its real-world effectiveness remains uncertain.

title: Walmart reveals soccer fans’ World Cup shopping habits
gist: Walmart released data on how its customers planned and executed grocery shopping around the FIFA World Cup, covering spend levels and product preferences. The findings confirm that major sporting events drive predictable, trackable shifts in consumer purchasing behavior.

title: ‘The store is a lemonade stand’: Brands are lending their retail spaces to other brands for events
gist: Independent retailers like P.F. Candle Co. and Sorbara's are renting or sharing their storefronts to other brands for events — matcha carts, book launches, pop-ups — to monetize square footage and reach new audiences. The trend reflects a broader pivot toward treating physical retail as experiential and collaborative rather than purely transactional.

title: Victoria’s Secret Pink’s Gen-Z-focused holiday strategy, with president Ali Dillon
gist: Victoria's Secret's Pink brand posted low-double-digit growth in Q1 2026 and is expanding beyond its core intimates and fleece into accessories, denim, and beauty to position itself as a full lifestyle brand for 18–24-year-old women. The holiday strategy centers on capturing Gen Z mall traffic through broader category reach and collaborations.

title: Marketplace Briefing: What’s driving a surge in Amazon sales for 3 startups
gist: Several digitally native startups have shifted away from DTC-first skepticism toward treating Amazon as a core growth channel, building deliberate flywheels around the platform's discovery and fulfillment advantages. The trend marks a reversal from the 2010s VC playbook that prioritized brand control over marketplace distribution.

title: Rather than going with the crowd, marketers are now questioning measurement ambiguity
gist: Marketers are increasingly acknowledging that multi-touch attribution creates a false sense of clarity, with multiple channels claiming credit for the same conversion while CFO and CMO numbers diverge. The piece argues the industry has collectively tolerated measurement ambiguity because challenging it is organizationally uncomfortable.

title: Uber’s $14.8B Delivery Hero deal would nearly double its global footprint
gist: Uber has agreed to acquire Delivery Hero for $14.8 billion in stock, which would expand Uber's delivery presence to nearly 100 markets globally, making it the largest delivery platform outside China. A separate deal sells Delivery Hero's operations in 14 Uber Eats-overlapping markets to SSW Partners for $1.6 billion.

title: QVC Group nears bankruptcy exit with approved restructuring plan
gist: QVC Group has received court approval for its Chapter 11 restructuring plan, cutting its debt from $6.6 billion to $1.3 billion with all vendor claims to be paid in full. The company is repositioning itself around live social shopping, including a 24/7 TikTok Shop livestream, as it prepares to relist as QVCG.

title: AI Makes Refund Evidence Easier to Fake
gist: Generative AI now enables fraudsters to fabricate convincing product-damage photos, fake shipping records, and forged customer-service conversations to support false refund claims, at minimal cost and effort. U.S. retailers processed roughly $849.9 billion in returns in 2025, with an estimated 9% fraudulent, and AI is expected to worsen that figure.

title: Fashion Briefing: Sneaker brands are slowing their drops to combat sneaker fatigue
gist: Major sneaker brands including Nike and Adidas are pulling back from the high-frequency drop cadence that dominated recent years, shifting toward fewer, higher-impact releases to counter 'sneaker fatigue' among collectors. The move signals a broader rethink of scarcity-and-hype as a sustainable sales strategy.

title: How Bloom Nutrition is expanding internationally
gist: Bloom Nutrition is expanding its energy drink line into Australia, France, and the U.K., navigating the cross-border friction that comes with international retail growth.

title: Back-to-school spending forecast to reach record highs
gist: Back-to-school spending is forecast to hit record highs this year — $43.3B for K-12 and over $100B for college — but shoppers are delaying purchases, hunting for deals, and spreading out spending due to budget pressure.

title: Ulta Beauty names Domino’s veteran as CTO
gist: Ulta Beauty has hired Domino's long-time CTO Kelly Garcia as its new chief technology officer, bringing over 25 years of experience in e-commerce, loyalty, and digital innovation to advance Ulta's tech-driven growth strategy.

title: Rakuten France is closing
gist: Rakuten France is shutting down its online marketplace by end of year after failing to find a buyer, citing disappointing traffic and sales.

title: CMO Doug Sweeny unpacks Oura’s winning bet on the World Cup — and what it means for his 2027 roadmap
gist: Oura's CMO shares how the brand leveraged the 2026 FIFA World Cup's record-breaking viewership and 20 billion social media views as a marketing platform, informing its 2027 strategy.

title: AI Mania Is Eviscerating Global Decision-Making
gist: A consultant argues that virtually all enterprise AI projects are failing, that executives are trapped in a game-theory dynamic where honesty about AI shortcomings is career-ending, and that organizations have become unable to make rational decisions because AI hype has become a loyalty test rather than a strategy.

title: NYC may require landlords and realtors to disclose the use of AI in listings
gist: New York City's mayor is proposing rules requiring landlords and realtors to disclose when listing photos have been altered or generated using AI, as part of a broader tenant-protection report.

title: The Kimi K3 Moment
gist: A developer finds Kimi K3, a Chinese open-source model, matches Claude's performance at roughly one-third the API cost and argues this exposes the failure of US AI export restrictions, which constrain American customers without limiting foreign competition.

title: ‘Bol and Amazon use misleading discounts’
gist: The Dutch Consumer Association has issued cease-and-desist letters to Bol and Amazon Netherlands, alleging the platforms display fake discounts that violate consumer protection law.

title: From season to sentiment: How the pace of consumer trends reshapes retail planning
gist: Retailers relying on seasonal planning frameworks are struggling to keep up as social media and viral moments create unpredictable, fragmented demand spikes that require real-time, unified data signals to manage.

title: AI is creating ecommerce’s next optimization challenge: The intent gap
gist: As AI tools like ChatGPT handle more of the pre-purchase research, shoppers increasingly arrive at retailer sites at different decision stages, creating an 'intent gap' where static checkout and cart experiences fail to match what each shopper actually needs.

title: WTF: W3C’s Attribution API?
gist: Following Google's retreat from third-party cookie deprecation, browser-based ad measurement is being re-debated within the W3C standards body, keeping the question of cookieless attribution alive for marketers and ad tech.

title: Guitar Center plots its turnaround, with a focus on serving ‘serious musicians’
gist: Guitar Center reports its ninth straight quarter of positive growth with sales up 5.2% to $635 million, crediting a strategic refocus on serious musicians and noting a 16% rise in in-store dwell time and the first store traffic increase in over a decade.

title: Reformation targets $1B valuation with IPO
gist: Reformation is pursuing an IPO targeting up to $1B valuation, pricing 14M+ shares at $15–$17, expecting ~$134.5M in net proceeds mostly used to repay debt. The brand posted $507M revenue and $12.6M net income in 2025, with over 30% of new DTC shoppers acquired through physical retail stores.

title: Zalando invests in AI robotics
gist: Zalando has taken an investment stake in German AI robotics firm Sereact to accelerate logistics automation, with a focus on returns processing.

title: German ecommerce increases 4.3% in first half 2026
gist: German e-commerce turnover grew 4.3% in H1 2026 year-over-year, accelerating to 5.1% growth in Q2, suggesting recovering consumer confidence in one of Europe's largest online markets.

title: Why brands like Untuckit are experimenting with robot-made T-shirts
gist: Some U.S. apparel brands, including Untuckit, are experimenting with AI-powered robotics to enable domestic manufacturing, as tariffs have so far failed to shift import patterns away from Asian countries.

title: GameStop discloses 10% eBay stake as it pushes for a deal
gist: GameStop has doubled its eBay stake to nearly 10% after eBay rejected its $56B acquisition bid in May, with CEO Ryan Cohen publicly signaling he will pursue the deal with or without board cooperation.

title: AliExpress hit with record $625M fine after failing to make EU-ordered fixes
gist: The European Commission fined AliExpress over $625M under the Digital Services Act — the largest DSA fine to date — for failing to adequately remove illegal, unsafe, and counterfeit products from its platform.

title: American Eagle is turning RushTok and malls into its back-to-school sales engine
gist: American Eagle's 2026 back-to-school campaign spans ~10 weeks with mall events, campus partnerships, and multiple talent deals including Sydney Sweeney and World Cup winner Lamine Yamal, deliberately moving away from a single monoculture campaign after last year's Sweeney push brought in 790,000 new customers.

title: Exclusive: Target aims to strengthen its tween business with new multi-year Pacsun partnership
gist: Target is launching a multi-year, 100+ style tween apparel line co-created with Pacsun, priced under $30, available monthly in stores and on Target.com — framed as a deeper, longer-term brand partnership than Target's typical limited collaborations.

title: Advertise in ChatGPT
gist: OpenAI has launched an advertising platform, ads.openai.com, opening ChatGPT to paid advertising placements.

title: Unpacked: How agentic AI is reshaping the consumer journey
gist: A sponsored Glossy guide argues that agentic AI is moving from concept to deployment in commerce, with Bain projecting agentic AI could represent 15–25% of total U.S. e-commerce by 2030 as platforms like Google and OpenAI embed agents into shopping flows.

title: Allegro Group GMV increases 14%
gist: Polish marketplace Allegro reported 13.7% GMV growth in H1 vs. the same period in 2025, with international GMV up 64.8%.

title: Brands Briefing: Why employee-generated content is a priority for brands like Starbucks and Mephisto
gist: Brands including Starbucks and Mephisto are making employee-generated content a deliberate strategy, with Mephisto USA explicitly hiring store staff who can also create daily social content and act as affiliates.

title: More brands are leaning on founders to drive social content
gist: Founder-led short-form video content is becoming an informal requirement for DTC brands, with retailers reportedly factoring a founder's social presence into wholesale decisions because it can drive in-store traffic.

title: Fashion Briefing: Canadian brands are investing in US fulfillment centers in wake of new tariffs
gist: Canadian apparel brands are accelerating investment in US-based fulfillment centers to sidestep new Trump tariffs, but the cost of local fulfillment is proving significant.

title: Kroger’s new CEO, a Walmart veteran, is focused on speed and execution
gist: Kroger's new CEO Greg Foran, a Walmart veteran, is emphasizing speed, execution, and financial discipline, with an early move being the $1.7B acquisition of Giant Eagle.

title: The back-to-school denim wars are starting earlier and lasting longer
gist: Major retailers including Pacsun, Kohl's, and Hollister are launching back-to-school denim campaigns earlier than ever, as NRF data shows 62% of shoppers had already started back-to-school shopping by early July, up from 55% in 2024.

title: Nike resets online distribution in ‘fragmented’ China market
gist: Nike is consolidating its China online presence from over 1,000 digital storefronts down to a handful of official channels on Tmall, JD.com, and Douyin, cutting major wholesale distributors from online sales amid a 12% revenue decline in the region.

title: New Ecommerce Tools: July 22, 2026
gist: This week's e-commerce tool roundup covers new launches including Squarespace drop-selling features, a DoorDash–Shopify integration for on-demand delivery, TikTok Shop's managed services pilot, and Dotdigital's AI loyalty platform.

title: 54% of shoppers report more shelf gaps since the start of Middle East conflict
gist: Research from invent.ai found that 54% of UK shoppers report worse on-shelf availability since Middle East conflict began, with 40% of planned grocery spend unavailable on average, and fashion shoppers particularly quick to switch brands or channels when facing stockouts.

title: Michaels says Google-powered AI assistant doubles conversion rate of traditional search
gist: Michaels' AI shopping assistant 'Ask Mike', built on Google Cloud's Gemini platform, has logged 75,000 conversations since its quiet May launch and reportedly doubles the conversion rate compared to traditional on-site search.

title: Michaels launches AI shopping assistant
gist: Michaels launched 'Ask Mike', an AI shopping assistant built with Google Cloud's Gemini platform in six weeks, offering personalized product recommendations and creative guidance, with plans to add AI-generated product overviews and contextual prompts.

title: How the shifting shopping calendar has become a customer acquisition opportunity
gist: Traditional retail peaks like Prime Day, Black Friday, and back-to-school have stretched into multi-week or multi-month events, reshaping how brands compete for customer acquisition across an increasingly elongated shopping calendar.

title: Marketplace Briefing: How Whatnot’s AI-powered recommendation system gives it a competitive edge
gist: Whatnot has reduced recommendation latency to minutes, allowing its system to ingest real-time signals like sold-out inventory from active livestreams and immediately adjust what products it surfaces to buyers.

title: What to know about new proposed tariffs on Canadian goods
gist: The Trump administration announced new tariffs on Canadian goods — covering autos, dairy, alcohol, and select consumer categories — set to take effect August 19, though negotiations could alter final rates before then.

title: Tractor Supply sputters in Q2, lowers outlook
gist: Tractor Supply posted a Q2 comp decline of 1.5% and cut its full-year outlook, citing drought, discretionary pullback, and encroachment by Amazon and Walmart into its rural customer base.

title: Google quietly gives ground on PMax controls
gist: Google is testing a feature that lets media buyers exclude third-party search partners and the Google Display Network from Performance Max campaigns, offering more spending control than the product has historically allowed.
